title: Online food delivery disputes - the consumer remedies available
p1:
Your food order arrived late, cold, or completely different from what you paid for. Do you simply accept the loss?

Many consumers don't realise they may have remedies beyond leaving a bad review.
p2: A common misconception is that once an order is placed through a delivery app, the consumer has no real option if something goes wrong. In reality, issues like missing items, poor service, unfair charges, or misleading claims can become consumer disputes.
p3:
The first step is always to keep proof: order details, payment records, photos, and conversations with customer support. Depending on the situation, consumers may seek a refund, replacement, compensation, or approach consumer grievance mechanisms. The key insight: digital transactions still come with consumer rights.

A small record today can make a big difference if a dispute arises tomorrow.
conclusion: Have you ever faced an online food delivery issue where the platform refused a fair solution? What remedy worked for you?
